    52→The problem asks "Is it maximised by taking the z_i to be the vertices of a regular polygon...?" — a yes/no question. Disproved for even n ≥ 4.
    53→- [x] Uses `answer(False) ↔ P` format (answer is "no").

    63→- [x] Uses `answer(sorry) ↔ P` (still open).

file.content:
# Review: `deepmind/1045.lean` — Erdős Problem 1045

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 1045`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link: `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1045](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1045)`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (maximum of Δ over configurations with pairwise distance ≤ 2).
- [x] Bibliography entry present: `[EHP58]` with full citation.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos1045 ... end Erdos1045`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos1045`).
- [x] `open Complex Finset BigOperators` appears **before** the namespace block (line 38, namespace begins line 40).

## 5. Custom Definitions

### `erdosDelta` (line 44)
- [x] Has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] Marked `noncomputable`.
- [x] No `sorry` in definition.
- [x] Uses `lowerCamelCase`.

### `regularNGon` (line 48)
- [x] Has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] Marked `noncomputable`.
- [x] No `sorry` in definition.
- [x] Uses `lowerCamelCase`.

No local notation used (none needed).

## 6. Theorem Statements

### `erdos_1045` (line 62)
- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Named `erdos_1045` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 30]`.
- [x] Has a docstring referencing `[EHP58]`.

### `answer()` usage — Case 1 (yes/no question)
The problem asks "Is it maximised by taking the z_i to be the vertices of a regular polygon...?" — a yes/no question. Disproved for even n ≥ 4.
- [x] Uses `answer(False) ↔ P` format (answer is "no").
- [x] RHS formalizes the question as originally asked (∀ n ≥ 1, ∀ z with pairwise distance ≤ 2, Δ(z) ≤ Δ(regularNGon)).
- [x] `answer(False)` correctly means "no" to the question.
- [x] Quantifiers appear **after** `answer(False)` (on the RHS of `↔`), not as theorem parameters.

### `erdos_1045.variants.odd` (line 72)
- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Named `erdos_1045.variants.odd` (correct variant naming convention).
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the same attribute line: `@[category research open, AMS 30]`.
- [x] Has a docstring explaining it is the restriction to odd n.
- [x] Uses `answer(sorry) ↔ P` (still open).
- [x] Quantifiers appear after `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] `erdos_1045`: exactly one `@[category research solved]` — valid value.
- [x] `erdos_1045.variants.odd`: exactly one `@[category research open]` — valid value.
- [x] The `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Both theorems have exactly one `@[AMS 30]`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed.
- [x] `30` (Functions of a complex variable) is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.
- [x] Single number — ascending order and no-duplicates rules trivially satisfied.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Both theorems use multi-line `by\n  sorry` format.
- [x] No `sorry` in `def` or `abbrev` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for the open variant.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- [x] Variant named `erdos_1045.variants.odd` (correct format).
- [x] Variant lives in the **same file** as the main theorem.
- [x] Variant has its own `@[category research open, AMS 30]` attributes (differs from main: `research open` vs `research solved`).
- [x] Variant has a brief docstring explaining the restriction to odd n.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `∀`, `≥`, `≤`, `ℕ`, `ℂ`, `ℝ`, `↔`, `≠`, `→`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all < 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` — not verified in this review.
- [ ] Custom linters — not verified in this review.

---

**Verdict:** All checkable items pass. The file is well-structured, with correct `answer()` usage (Case 1 yes/no: `answer(False)` for the disproved general conjecture, `answer(sorry)` for the still-open odd case), proper category/AMS attributes, docstrings with references, and clean code quality. Build verification remains to be confirmed.

content.text: The review passed — all checkable items have checkmarks, and the verdict says "All checkable items pass." No changes needed.
